brands.name: RooterMan
brands.notes: Plumbing, sewer, and drain-cleaning franchise with one of the lowest entry costs in home services ($4,975 franchise fee) and a flat population-based royalty instead of a percentage of sales. Acquired by multi-brand platform Premium Service Brands (Charlottesville, VA) in 2021; ~690 franchised territories and zero company-owned units.

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 financial performance representation
riskFlags.detail: Rooter-Man does not make an Item 19 financial performance representation in its FDD, so buyers get no franchisor-published revenue or profit data despite a ~690-unit system.
riskFlags.source: FranchiseOverview Rooter-Man FDD summary

riskFlags.title: High churn under the flat-fee model (91 closures in 2021)
riskFlags.detail: FDD outlet tables show 99 openings against 91 closures in 2021 - heavy turnover consistent with a low-cost, low-overhead license model where marginal operators exit easily.
riskFlags.source: FranchiseOverview Rooter-Man FDD summary (outlet tables)

riskFlags.title: Acquired by PE-backed multi-brand platform Premium Service Brands (2021)
riskFlags.detail: RooterMan is now one of nine-plus home-service brands under Premium Service Brands, which restructured training and operations after the acquisition; legacy franchisees signed under the founder-era Massachusetts organization operate under a changed corporate parent.
riskFlags.source: Premium Service Brands plumbing franchise pages

riskFlags.title: Population-priced royalty is owed regardless of revenue
riskFlags.detail: Royalty is $3 per 1,000 of territory population per month plus $1 per 1,000 population to the ad fund - a fixed obligation independent of actual sales, which cuts both ways: cheap for high-volume operators, a fixed drag for slow starters.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Direct Rooter-Man FDD summary; FranchiseOverview

totalInvestmentMin.source: Premium Service Brands RooterMan cost page (franchisor); FranchiseOverview FDD summary

totalInvestmentMax.source: Premium Service Brands RooterMan cost page (franchisor); FranchiseOverview FDD summary

franchiseFee.source: Premium Service Brands RooterMan cost page (franchisor)
franchiseFee.note: One of the lowest initial fees among Franchise 500 home-service brands.

totalUnits.note: All franchised; 0 company-owned. Counts territories/licenses rather than storefronts.

yearFounded.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (RooterMan); franchisor cites 50+ years of service

trainingWeeks.source: FranchiseOverview FDD summary; Premium Service Brands (month-long three-part program)
trainingWeeks.note: 76 hours classroom plus on-the-job and online onboarding, virtual and in Charlottesville, VA.

renewalTermYears.note: 10-year initial term with 2 additional successive 10-year renewal terms.

financingOffered.note: Neither franchisor nor affiliates finance the initial investment.

bankruptcyDisclosed.source: FranchiseOverview Rooter-Man FDD summary (no litigation or bankruptcy disclosed)

businessModelType.source: RooterMan franchisor site (residential and commercial plumbing/drain service)

fddAvailable.source: Franchise Direct and FranchiseOverview FDD summaries

brands.name: ComForCare
brands.notes: Non-medical senior home care franchise under Best Life Brands (Troy, MI), with 248 franchised territories and steady growth (+20 net units in 2024). Item 19 economics are strongly right-skewed: the median territory does roughly $806K while the average is pulled to $1.29M by a handful of very large operators. Revenue mix spans private pay (44%), Medicaid/state programs (24%), insurance (13%), and veterans programs (13%).

riskFlags.title: Average gross sales overstate the typical unit by ~60%
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19: average territory gross sales were $1,290,448 but the median was $805,787, and only 32% of territories met or exceeded the average (range $40,384-$18,434,636). The mean is inflated by a few outliers up to $18.4M.
riskFlags.source: ComForCare 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

riskFlags.title: Majority of revenue depends on third-party payers
riskFlags.detail: Only 44% of 2024 system revenue was private pay; Medicaid/state programs (24%), insurance (13%), and veterans programs (13%) together exceed half of revenue, exposing franchisees to government reimbursement rates, payer credentialing, and program funding changes.
riskFlags.source: ComForCare 2025 FDD revenue-mix data (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

riskFlags.title: Stacked ongoing fees can reach 9%+ of gross sales
riskFlags.detail: Royalty of 5% (standard) or 6-7% (reduced-fee offering) with minimum monthly royalties, plus 1% general service fee, 1% national advertising, and a required 2% local marketing spend - combined ongoing obligations of roughly 9-11% of gross sales.
riskFlags.source: ComForCare FDD fee disclosures (via Franchise Chatter)

riskFlags.title: One brand inside PE-backed Best Life Brands platform
riskFlags.detail: ComForCare sits in the Best Life Brands portfolio (with At Your Side, CarePatrol, Blue Moon Estate Sales, Boost Home Healthcare), a private-equity-backed multi-brand senior-services platform that sets shared-services and development priorities across brands.
riskFlags.source: Franchise Chatter 2026 review; Best Life Brands corporate site

franchiseFee.note: Standard offering $59,000; Reduced Initial Fee offering $29,500 with a higher 6-7% royalty.

royaltyPercent.note: 5% of gross sales (standard offering) with minimum monthly royalty; 6-7% under the reduced-initial-fee offering.

adFundPercent.note: 1% national advertising plus 1% general service fee and required 2% local marketing spend.

avgUnitRevenue.source: ComForCare 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average gross sales across 202 territories; median $805,787. Per-owner average $1,918,396 across 148 owners (many own multiple territories).

totalUnits.source: ComForCare 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
totalUnits.note: All franchised; 0 company-owned at end of 2024.

yearFounded.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Franchise Direct ComForCare summary

yearFranchising.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Franchise Direct ComForCare summary

isGrowing.source: ComForCare FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

mobileBased.source: ComForCare model (small office; care delivered in client homes)

brands.name: Uptown Cheapskate
brands.notes: Fashion-resale (buy/sell/trade used clothing) franchise from BaseCamp Franchising (Salt Lake City; also franchises Kid to Kid), riding the secondhand-apparel boom with ~143 stores and consistent double-digit annual openings. Unusually transparent Item 19: publishes both sales AND net income by quartile - system average $1.28M sales / $188K net income (14.7%).

riskFlags.title: Bottom-quartile stores earn $55K net on $707K sales
riskFlags.detail: 2025 FDD Item 19 (Nov 2023-Oct 2024): bottom-quartile average gross sales were $707,320 with net income of $55,489 (7.8% margin) versus $1,932,305 and $354,052 (18.3%) for the top quartile - a near 3x sales spread on an investment that can exceed $596K.
riskFlags.source: Uptown Cheapskate 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

riskFlags.title: Fixed $2,000/month minimum local advertising on top of percentage fees
riskFlags.detail: Beyond the 5% royalty and 0.5% ad fund, franchisees must spend a minimum of $2,000 per month on local advertising - a fixed obligation regardless of store revenue, hitting hardest during ramp-up.
riskFlags.source: Uptown Cheapskate FDD fee disclosures (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

riskFlags.title: Working-capital-heavy model: investment range tops out near $600K
riskFlags.detail: Item 7 runs $328,002-$596,502, a wide range driven by inventory acquisition (stores buy used clothing from the public for cash) and large-format retail build-out - materially more than typical resale concepts and sensitive to local real-estate costs.
riskFlags.source: Uptown Cheapskate 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter and Entrepreneur)

liquidCapitalMin.note: SHARPSHEETS/franchisor recruiting materials cite $150,000 liquid and $400,000 net worth for some markets.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Uptown Cheapskate 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review; Entrepreneur)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Uptown Cheapskate 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review; Entrepreneur)

franchiseFee.source: Uptown Cheapskate 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review; Entrepreneur)

royaltyPercent.source: Uptown Cheapskate 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

adFundPercent.source: Uptown Cheapskate 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
adFundPercent.note: Plus minimum $2,000/month required local advertising spend.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Uptown Cheapskate 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Average gross sales across 121 reporting stores (Nov 2023-Oct 2024); average net income $188,356 (14.7%). Franchisor marketing cites $1.4M sales / $227K net income for more recent periods.

totalUnits.source: Uptown Cheapskate 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
totalUnits.note: 129 franchised + 14 company-owned at end of 2024; Entrepreneur lists 152 units in 2026.

isGrowing.source: Uptown Cheapskate FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

mobileBased.source: Uptown Cheapskate franchisor site (retail storefront model)

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter 2026 FDD review (sales and net income by quartile)

brands.name: Floyd's 99 Barbershop
brands.notes: Rock-n-roll-themed 'amplified' barbershop chain out of Greenwood Village, CO (founded by the Eccardt brothers in 1999; franchising since 2005) with ~136-138 shops. Targets experienced multi-unit operators: $500K liquidity and $1.5M net worth required, with development agreements rather than single-shop deals as the standard path. AUV of ~$954K is more than double the haircare sub-sector average.

riskFlags.title: Litigation and/or bankruptcy disclosures in the FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services flag that Floyd's 99 discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD (Items 3/4) - buyers should read those items closely and ask the franchisor for specifics before signing.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Floyd's 99 Barbershop Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Steep financial bar: $500K liquid / $1.5M net worth, multi-unit expected
riskFlags.detail: The franchisor's own FAQ requires minimum liquidity of $500,000 and net worth of at least $1.5 million, with development fees structured for multi-shop agreements ($49,500 each for shops one and two, $34,500 each thereafter) and a requirement that candidates live in the market they develop.
riskFlags.source: Floyd's 99 Franchising FAQ (franchisor site)

riskFlags.title: Modest system size after 20 years of franchising
riskFlags.detail: Roughly 136-138 shops total after franchising since 2005 - slow absolute growth for a 25-year-old brand, and a meaningful share of the system is company-operated, so the franchised footprint is smaller still. Labor availability for licensed barbers/stylists is the operating constraint in this category.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS Floyd's 99 FDD analysis; Vetted Biz

liquidCapitalMin.note: Vetted Biz lists a lower $100,000 figure; the franchisor's current FAQ states $500,000 minimum liquidity for development candidates.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Floyd's 99 Franchising FAQ (franchisor site); Vetted Biz

totalInvestmentMax.source: Floyd's 99 Franchising FAQ (franchisor site); Vetted Biz

franchiseFee.source: Floyd's 99 Franchising FAQ (development fee per shop for first two shops); Vetted Biz
franchiseFee.note: $49,500 each for first and second barbershops, $34,500 for each additional shop under a multi-unit development agreement.

royaltyPercent.source: Vetted Biz - Floyd's 99 Barbershop Franchise Insights

adFundPercent.source: Vetted Biz - Floyd's 99 Barbershop Franchise Insights

avgUnitRevenue.source: Floyd's 99 FDD Item 19 (via Vetted Biz and SHARPSHEETS analyses)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Yearly gross sales; estimated owner earnings $133,582-$171,749 per Vetted Biz. SHARPSHEETS cites $979,050 vs sub-sector average of $404,662.

totalUnits.source: Vetted Biz - Floyd's 99 Barbershop Franchise Insights
totalUnits.note: US locations including company-operated shops; SHARPSHEETS cites 136.

yearFounded.source: SHARPSHEETS Floyd's 99 FDD analysis (25 years since 1999)
yearFounded.note: Vetted Biz lists 2001; brand history cites first Denver shop in 1999.

yearFranchising.source: Vetted Biz - Floyd's 99 Barbershop Franchise Insights

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz - Floyd's 99 Barbershop Franchise Insights
trainingWeeks.note: Two weeks at Colorado headquarters plus stylist/barber technical training programs.

thirdPartyFinancing.value: No direct financing; franchisor has partnerships with several preferred lenders

multiUnitAvailable.source: Floyd's 99 Franchising FAQ (multi-unit development agreements standard)

brands.name: Mr. Appliance
brands.notes: Appliance-repair franchise in the Neighborly portfolio (Waco, TX), ~310 franchised territories and zero company-owned units. Low-overhead van-based model, but note the FDD's Item 19 discloses average revenue PER JOB ($295) rather than annual unit revenue - third-party analysts estimate ~$422K average yearly gross sales - and the system shed 15 net units in 2024.

riskFlags.title: System contracted by 15 net units in 2024
riskFlags.detail: FDD outlet tables show franchised units went 315->325 (2022), flat in 2023, then 325->310 in 2024 - a roughly 4.6% one-year decline for a 30-year-old brand inside the heavily marketed Neighborly platform.
riskFlags.source: Mr. Appliance 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

riskFlags.title: Item 19 discloses per-job averages, not annual revenue
riskFlags.detail: The FDD's financial performance representation is average gross sales per JOB ($295 average / $286 median across 289 reporting units in 2024) - it does not publish annual unit revenue, making earnings diligence dependent on third-party estimates (~$422K AUV) and franchisee validation calls.
riskFlags.source: Mr. Appliance 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review); SHARPSHEETS estimate

riskFlags.title: Stacked fees: 5-7% royalty + 2% MAP + up to 2% local marketing groups
riskFlags.detail: The license fee runs 5-7% of gross sales depending on volume, plus a 2% Marketing/Advertising/Promotion fee and local marketing group contributions of up to 2% - combined ongoing obligations approaching 9-11% of gross sales, and the population-priced franchise fee adds $425 per 1,000 people over the 125,000 minimum territory.
riskFlags.source: Mr. Appliance 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

nationwide.source: Neighborly - Mr. Appliance franchise page (territory availability)
nationwide.note: Neighborly markets Mr. Appliance territories across the US via an availability checker, with standard franchise-registration-state disclaimers. Confirm specific territory availability with Neighborly.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Mr. Appliance 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Excludes real estate; van-based home model.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Mr. Appliance 2025 FDD Item 7 (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)

franchiseFee.source: Mr. Appliance 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter and SHARPSHEETS)
franchiseFee.note: Plus $425 per 1,000 population over the 125,000 minimum territory.

royaltyPercent.source: Mr. Appliance 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
royaltyPercent.note: License fee of 5-7% of gross sales depending on revenue tier.

adFundPercent.source: Mr. Appliance 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
adFundPercent.note: 2% MAP fee plus local marketing group contributions up to 2%.

avgUnitRevenue.source: SHARPSHEETS Mr. Appliance FDD analysis (third-party estimate)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Estimated yearly gross sales; FDD Item 19 only discloses per-job averages ($295 avg / $286 median, 2024). Estimated earnings $50,694-$63,368 per SHARPSHEETS.

totalUnits.source: Mr. Appliance 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
totalUnits.note: All franchised; 0 company-owned at end of 2024.

yearFounded.source: Vetted Biz - Mr. Appliance Franchise Insights; Entrepreneur directory

yearFranchising.source: Vetted Biz - Mr. Appliance Franchise Insights; Entrepreneur directory

isGrowing.source: Mr. Appliance 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter 2026 review)
isGrowing.note: -15 net franchised units in 2024 after a flat 2023.

businessModelType.source: Mr. Appliance franchisor site (residential and light commercial appliance repair)

mobileBased.source: Mr. Appliance van-based service model (Neighborly franchise page)

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter 2026 FDD review (per-job revenue only)
item19Available.note: Limited: per-job averages, not annual unit revenue.

brands.name: Griswold
brands.notes: One of the oldest non-medical home care franchises in the US (founded by Jean Griswold in 1982, Blue Bell, PA), now ~110-130 territories. Best-in-category unit economics: 2024 average revenue of $2.13M (median $1.67M) with low ongoing fees (4% royalty). Smaller system than rivals like Home Instead or ComForCare, and the FDD contains litigation disclosures buyers should review.

riskFlags.title: Lawsuits and/or bankruptcy disclosed in the FDD
riskFlags.detail: FDD-tracking services flag that Griswold discloses lawsuits and/or bankruptcy information in its FDD (Items 3/4); prospective buyers should obtain the current FDD and review the disclosed matters before signing.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Griswold Home Care Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Average revenue runs ~27% above the median
riskFlags.detail: 2024 system average revenue was $2,131,036 versus a median of $1,672,644 - large established territories pull the mean up, so a typical new franchisee should anchor on the median or below during ramp-up.
riskFlags.source: Griswold 2025 FDD Item 19 (via SHARPSHEETS FDD analysis)

riskFlags.title: Caregiver labor supply and state non-medical care regulation are the operating constraints
riskFlags.detail: Like all senior home care systems, revenue capacity is gated by caregiver recruiting/retention in a tight labor market and by state-level home care licensing; 16 of ~110 locations are company-owned, so the franchisor also competes for caregivers in its own markets.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS Griswold FDD analysis (unit mix); industry context

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Griswold Home Care Franchise Insights (minimum cash required)
liquidCapitalMin.note: Directory figure; franchisor recruiting materials typically require substantially more liquidity for working capital.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Griswold 2025 FDD Item 7 (via The FDD Exchange and Vetted Biz)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Includes $49,500-$54,500 paid to Griswold International, LLC or affiliates.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Griswold 2025 FDD Item 7 (via The FDD Exchange and Vetted Biz)

franchiseFee.source: Griswold 2025 FDD (via The FDD Exchange and Vetted Biz)

royaltyPercent.source: Griswold 2025 FDD (via The FDD Exchange and SHARPSHEETS)

adFundPercent.note: Greater of $75 or 0.5% of gross receipts to national marketing; Vetted Biz cites 1%.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Griswold 2025 FDD Item 19 (via SHARPSHEETS FDD analysis)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average revenue; median $1,672,644. SHARPSHEETS estimates owner earnings of $301,076-$418,161.

totalUnits.note: 94 franchised + 16 company-owned; Vetted Biz cites 130 franchised US locations.

yearFounded.source: Griswold franchisor site brand history (founded by Jean Griswold); Franchise Direct
yearFounded.note: Vetted Biz lists 2009, which reflects the current franchising entity rather than the 1982 founding.

yearFranchising.source: Franchise Direct Griswold summary; franchisor brand history

trainingWeeks.source: Vetted Biz - Griswold Home Care Franchise Insights
trainingWeeks.note: Concentrated five-day initial program at Pennsylvania headquarters plus ongoing support.

mobileBased.source: Griswold model (small office; care delivered in client homes)

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (Griswold 2025 FDD); Vetted Biz

brands.name: Celebree School
brands.notes: Early-childhood education / daycare franchise founded 1994 in Maryland by Richard Huffman; corporate-proven (26 company schools) but young as a franchise system (franchising since 2019, 29 franchised schools in the 2025 FDD). Real-estate-heavy investment that can exceed $5.6M for ground-up builds, offset by landlord/developer build-to-suit options in some markets.

riskFlags.title: Investment range tops out at $5.6M
riskFlags.detail: Item 7 runs roughly $923,000-$5,625,000 depending on whether the school is a leased fit-out or ground-up development - among the heaviest investments in child care franchising, with long build timelines and enrollment ramp before break-even.
riskFlags.source: Celebree School 2025 FDD Item 7 (via SHARPSHEETS FDD analysis)

riskFlags.title: Headline AUV comes from company-operated schools, not franchisees
riskFlags.detail: The $2.26M AUV cited in the FDD reflects company-operated schools open throughout the year; the franchised base (29 units, franchising only since 2019) is young, so mature franchisee economics are not yet well evidenced.
riskFlags.source: Celebree School FDD Item 19 (via SHARPSHEETS analysis); FranchisePayback

riskFlags.title: State childcare licensing and staffing ratios gate revenue
riskFlags.detail: Licensed-capacity rules, teacher-child ratios, and persistent early-childhood educator shortages cap enrollment and drive labor costs; schools cannot legally enroll beyond licensed capacity regardless of demand.
riskFlags.source: Celebree School franchise materials (franchising.com profile); industry context

riskFlags.title: Combined ongoing fees of ~9% of gross revenue
riskFlags.detail: 7% royalty plus 1% national marketing fund plus a required minimum 1% local marketing spend - roughly 9% of gross revenue in ongoing fees, on a labor-intensive ~15% operating-margin business per third-party estimates.
riskFlags.source: Celebree School 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS analysis; FranchisePayback)

totalInvestmentMin.source: Celebree School 2025 FDD Item 7 (via SHARPSHEETS FDD analysis)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Celebree School 2025 FDD Item 7 (via SHARPSHEETS FDD analysis)

royaltyPercent.source: Celebree School 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and FranchisePayback)

adFundPercent.note: 1% national marketing fund plus minimum 1% local marketing; FranchisePayback aggregates total advertising obligations at 4%.

avgUnitRevenue.note: FDD cites $2.26M AUV for company-operated schools open throughout 2025; SHARPSHEETS-derived yearly gross sales $1,981,871 with ~15.5% operating profit.

yearFounded.source: FranchisePayback; Celebree franchisor site brand history

trainingWeeks.note: Initial 3-week classroom/web/on-site program plus up to 8 days pre-opening in-territory support.

isGrowing.source: FranchisePayback (rapid franchised-unit growth from a small base); Celebree development incentive program announcements
isGrowing.note: Young system growing fast in percentage terms; franchisor launched a development incentive program for new owners.

item19Available.source: SHARPSHEETS Celebree FDD analysis (company-operated school AUV disclosed)

brands.name: Conserva Irrigation
brands.notes: Water-efficiency-focused irrigation repair and smart-sprinkler franchise (Glen Allen, VA) in the Empower Brands portfolio, ~202 territories. System sales grew from $6.5M (2018) to $46.8M (2024), but growth slowed sharply to +7 net units in 2024 and the median territory does $440K - well below the $672K average, which is lifted by multi-territory operators.

riskFlags.title: Median revenue is 35% below the headline average
riskFlags.detail: 2024 Item 19: average gross revenue across 68 franchisees (172 territories) was $671,826 versus a median of $439,503 (range $91,348-$4,677,021); 92% of the 13 top performers above $1.1M were multi-territory operators, so single-territory expectations should anchor near the median.
riskFlags.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2025 review)

riskFlags.title: Mandatory $40,000+/year individual advertising investment
riskFlags.detail: Beyond the tiered 5-8% royalty and 1.5% national brand fee, the FDD requires a minimum individual advertising investment of $40,000 per calendar year for one territory ($60,000 for two contiguous territories) plus a $300/month digital marketing fee - a heavy fixed obligation against a ~$440K median revenue.
riskFlags.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD (via The FDD Exchange and Franchise Chatter)

riskFlags.title: Growth decelerated to +7 net units in 2024
riskFlags.detail: Franchised territories grew +25 (2022) and +27 (2023) but only +7 in 2024 to 202 - a marked slowdown for a young system inside the PE-backed Empower Brands platform (which also owns Superior Fence & Rail, Outdoor Lighting Perspectives, and others).
riskFlags.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter 2025 review)

riskFlags.title: Seasonal business in most climates
riskFlags.detail: Irrigation startup/winterization cycles concentrate revenue in spring-fall in northern markets; royalty minimums ($1,000/month after 24 months) still apply in the off-season.
riskFlags.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD royalty minimums (via Franchise Chatter); business model

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Conserva Irrigation Franchise Insights
liquidCapitalMin.note: Directory minimum-cash figure; franchisor recruiting materials typically require more for the $40K/yr ad commitment.

totalInvestmentMin.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD Item 7 (via The FDD Exchange and Franchise Chatter)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Includes $49,500 paid to franchisor or affiliate.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD Item 7 (via The FDD Exchange and Franchise Chatter)

franchiseFee.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2025 review)

royaltyPercent.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD (via The FDD Exchange and Franchise Chatter)
royaltyPercent.note: Tiered: 8% up to $499,999 gross revenue, 7% to $999,999, 6% to $1,499,999, 5% above $1.5M; $1,000/month minimum after 24 months.

adFundPercent.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD (via The FDD Exchange)
adFundPercent.note: 1.5% national branding/marketing fund (on first $1.5M of revenue) plus $300/month digital marketing fee and $40,000+/year required individual ad spend.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2025 review)
avgUnitRevenue.note: 2024 average gross revenue per franchisee (68 franchisees / 172 territories); median $439,503. Franchisees 2+ years in (2023): average revenue-less-certain-expenses $164,538 (20.3%).

totalUnits.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter); Vetted Biz
totalUnits.note: All franchised; company-owned wound down from 5 (2022) to 0.

yearFranchising.source: Conserva Irrigation franchisor site (irrigationfranchise.com); Vetted Biz lists 2012
yearFranchising.note: Sources conflict; system sales data begins 2018, consistent with franchising launch circa 2017.

trainingWeeks.note: Two-week in-person program at Virginia headquarters.

isGrowing.source: Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD outlet tables (via Franchise Chatter)
isGrowing.note: Still adding units but decelerating: +25, +27, +7 across 2022-2024.

bankruptcyDisclosed.source: Vetted Biz (no lawsuits or bankruptcy disclosed in FDD)

businessModelType.source: Conserva Irrigation franchisor site (residential and commercial irrigation)

mobileBased.source: Conserva Irrigation van-based service model (franchisor site)

seasonalityNotes.value: Spring-fall concentrated in northern climates (startup/winterization cycles); year-round in Sun Belt markets

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (Conserva Irrigation 2025 FDD); Franchise Chatter

brands.name: Perkins American Food Co.
brands.notes: Legacy family-dining chain (founded 1958) rebranded from Perkins Restaurant & Bakery to 'Perkins American Food Co.' in 2024 under parent Ascent Hospitality Management (which also owns Huddle House). ~254-260 units split between franchised (~175) and company-owned (~82), with a shrinking footprint (-8% unit trend) offset by a new fast-casual 'Griddle & Go' format push. Parent emerged from the 2019 Perkins & Marie Callender's bankruptcy era - the brand was bought out of Chapter 11 by Huddle House in 2019.

riskFlags.title: System is shrinking (-8% unit trend)
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur's 2026 directory shows 254 units with a -8% trend, continuing a long decline for the 65-year-old family-dining brand as the category loses traffic to fast casual; payback estimates run 13-15 years on a $1.3M-$3.6M build.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; SHARPSHEETS Perkins FDD analysis

riskFlags.title: Brand sold out of Chapter 11 bankruptcy in 2019
riskFlags.detail: Perkins & Marie Callender's LLC filed Chapter 11 in August 2019 and the Perkins brand was acquired by Huddle House (Ascent Hospitality Management); buyers should review FDD Item 4 bankruptcy disclosures and the post-bankruptcy ownership structure.
riskFlags.source: Perkins & Marie Callender's Chapter 11 filing coverage; Ascent Hospitality history

riskFlags.title: Heavy build cost against modest AUV
riskFlags.detail: Total investment of $1,313,890-$3,581,375 against ~$1.94M average gross sales and estimated earnings of $135K-$194K per SHARPSHEETS - thin returns relative to capital unless real estate is favorable; the new smaller Griddle & Go format is the franchisor's answer.
riskFlags.source: Perkins 2025 FDD (via SHARPSHEETS and FranchisePayback)

riskFlags.title: Rebrand and format pivot still unproven
riskFlags.detail: The 2024 'Perkins American Food Co.' rebrand and fast-casual Griddle & Go spin-off (10-unit New Jersey deal signed 2025) are early-stage bets; franchisees are buying into a turnaround story rather than a stable concept.
riskFlags.source: Restaurant Dive - Perkins Griddle & Go 10-unit agreement

totalInvestmentMin.source: Perkins 2025 FDD Item 7 (via SHARPSHEETS and Entrepreneur)

totalInvestmentMax.source: Perkins 2025 FDD Item 7 (via SHARPSHEETS and Entrepreneur)

adFundPercent.note: 3% of net sales, may increase to 4%; 1% for non-traditional units in host facilities.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Perkins FDD-derived yearly gross sales (via SHARPSHEETS analysis)
avgUnitRevenue.note: Estimated earnings $135,510-$193,585; payback 13.2-15.2 years per SHARPSHEETS.

totalUnits.source: SHARPSHEETS Perkins FDD analysis (175 franchised + 82 company-owned); Entrepreneur lists 254

thirdPartyFinancing.value: Third-party financing available per Entrepreneur directory

bankruptcyDisclosed.source: Perkins & Marie Callender's 2019 Chapter 11 (Restaurant Dive coverage); predecessor entity
bankruptcyDisclosed.note: 2019 Chapter 11 of predecessor Perkins & Marie Callender's; brand acquired by Huddle House/Ascent.

fddAvailable.source: SHARPSHEETS; FranchisePayback; Franchise Direct FDD summaries

item19Available.source: SHARPSHEETS Perkins FDD analysis (gross sales disclosed)

brands.name: Rosati's Pizza (Rosati's Pizza Enterprises)
brands.notes: Chicago-style pizza brand dating to 1964, franchised through Rosati's Pizza Enterprises since 2006. The broader Rosati's name spans family-run and licensed locations beyond the FDD entity, so unit counts differ by source (FDD outlet tables ~63; Entrepreneur lists 133). Carryout/delivery-focused 'Express' formats keep entry costs near $312K while sports-bar formats exceed $1.4M.

riskFlags.title: Thin unit economics: ~$612K average sales, $43K-$61K estimated earnings
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived yearly gross sales of $612,355 with estimated owner earnings of $42,865-$61,236 and a 6.7-8.7 year payback - and the larger bar/restaurant formats cost up to $1.485M, which the average unit's volume does not comfortably support.
riskFlags.source: Rosati's Pizza 2025 FDD-derived analysis (SHARPSHEETS)

riskFlags.title: Unit counts vary widely by source (63 vs 133)
riskFlags.detail: The 2025 FDD outlet tables show 63 units (48 franchised + 15 company-owned) for the franchising entity, while Entrepreneur lists 133 units - the gap reflects legacy family-owned and licensed Rosati's locations outside the FDD system. Buyers should clarify exactly which system and support structure they are joining.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS FDD analysis vs Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory

riskFlags.title: Crowded category with national delivery price competition
riskFlags.detail: Independent and big-three pizza competition plus third-party delivery economics squeeze carryout/delivery margins; Rosati's regional brand strength is concentrated in the Chicago metro and Sun Belt transplant markets, with limited national ad fund support ($350/month / ~1%).
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur directory fee disclosures; SHARPSHEETS category analysis

totalInvestmentMin.source: Rosati's Pizza 2025 FDD Item 7 (via SHARPSHEETS and The FDD Exchange)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Entrepreneur lists $147,200 low end for the smallest Express format.

totalInvestmentMax.source: Rosati's Pizza 2025 FDD Item 7 (via SHARPSHEETS and The FDD Exchange)
totalInvestmentMax.note: Top end covers full sports-bar format; Entrepreneur lists $1,249,000.

adFundPercent.note: Entrepreneur lists a $350/month ad obligation and a ~1% ad royalty; SHARPSHEETS cites 2% marketing. Confirm in current FDD.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Rosati's Pizza FDD-derived yearly gross sales (SHARPSHEETS)

totalUnits.source: Rosati's Pizza 2025 FDD outlet tables (via SHARPSHEETS)
totalUnits.note: 48 franchised + 15 company-owned in the FDD entity; Entrepreneur lists 133 units (+7.3%) including legacy/licensed Rosati's locations.

thirdPartyFinancing.value: Established relationships with various financing and supply vendors

isGrowing.note: Entrepreneur trend positive; FDD-entity base remains small.

brands.name: Tide Cleaners
brands.notes: Dry-cleaning and laundry franchise leveraging Procter & Gamble's Tide brand, franchised by P&G subsidiary Agile Pursuits Franchising (Cincinnati). ~230-246 US locations across multiple formats - drop stores, lockers, plant stores, and the newer Tide Laundromat concept - which is why investment spans roughly $40K (locker/drop) to $2.5M (full plant). Capital-heavy formats carry a 12-14 year estimated payback against ~$683K average revenue.

riskFlags.title: Long payback: ~$683K AUV against builds up to $2.5M
riskFlags.detail: Vetted Biz estimates average unit revenue of $683,443 and owner earnings of $95,682-$123,020, implying a 12.2-14.2 year payback on full-format stores costing up to $2,517,300 - among the longest paybacks in the services category.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz - Tide Cleaners Franchise Insights

riskFlags.title: Secular decline in dry-cleaning demand
riskFlags.detail: US dry-cleaning volume never fully recovered from remote/hybrid work; the franchisor's pivot toward Tide Laundromat (2025 FDD shows $1.12M average laundromat revenue) is an acknowledgment that classic garment-care demand is structurally weaker, and existing dry-clean-format franchisees bear that shift.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS Tide Cleaners/Tide Laundromat FDD analyses

riskFlags.title: Franchisor is a P&G subsidiary with portfolio discretion
riskFlags.detail: Agile Pursuits Franchising is a unit of Procter & Gamble, which licenses the Tide mark; P&G's strategic priorities (brand protection, format pivots, corporate exits from ventures) sit above franchisee interests, and the brand license is the system's core asset.
riskFlags.source: Tide franchise site (franchisor); SHARPSHEETS

riskFlags.title: Stacked fees: 6.5-7% royalty plus ~4% marketing
riskFlags.detail: Royalty disclosures run 6.5% (SHARPSHEETS, weekly net sales) to 7% (Vetted Biz) plus roughly 4% total marketing (2% national + 2% local) - a high combined take for a margin-tight garment-care business.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS Tide Cleaners FDD analysis; Vetted Biz

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Tide Cleaners Franchise Insights

totalInvestmentMin.note: Full-store format; Vetted Biz lists $40,320 low end for locker/drop formats.

avgUnitRevenue.note: Tide Laundromat format averages $1,119,889 per the 2025 Laundromat FDD (SHARPSHEETS/FranDB).

brands.name: Bubbakoo's Burritos
brands.notes: Jersey Shore-born fusion burrito chain (founded by Paul Altero and Bill Hart; franchising since 2015) blending Mexican fare with skate/surf branding, ~130 locations concentrated in the Northeast. Solid mid-pack fast-casual economics: ~$825K-$903K average gross sales on a $356K-$757K build, ~5.5-7.5 year estimated payback, and a clean FDD litigation record.

riskFlags.title: Average unit volume trails fast-casual burrito leaders
riskFlags.detail: Average franchisee gross sales of ~$903K (top performers $1.98M; SHARPSHEETS-derived $825,288) are roughly a third of Chipotle-class volumes; on a build that can reach $757K, below-average stores face extended payback.
riskFlags.source: Bubbakoo's Burritos 2025 FDD-derived analyses (SHARPSHEETS; franchisor investment page)

riskFlags.title: Geographic concentration in New Jersey/Northeast
riskFlags.detail: The system's footprint is heavily weighted to New Jersey and adjacent states where the brand was born; franchisees entering new markets get less brand recognition than the home-market numbers imply.
riskFlags.source: Bubbakoo's locations footprint (franchisor site); Vetted Biz

riskFlags.title: Fast growth from a young base
riskFlags.detail: Roughly 130 units a decade after franchising began (2015), with 12 company-owned - rapid percentage growth means many stores and multi-unit operators are unseasoned, and mature-market saturation behavior is untested.
riskFlags.source: Vetted Biz; SHARPSHEETS outlet data (118 franchised + 12 company-owned, 2025 FDD)

liquidCapitalMin.source: Vetted Biz - Bubbakoo's Burritos Franchise Insights (minimum cash)

brands.name: Techy
brands.notes: Electronics repair and sales franchise (cell phones, computers, tablets, smart home) out of South Florida, formerly branded Dr Phone Fix, franchising under Techy LLC. Explosive unit growth (+173.8% per Entrepreneur, ~167 units) from a very young base, but FDD-derived average sales of ~$243K per store are among the lowest in this batch - fee minimums bite hard at that volume.

riskFlags.title: Very low average store revenue (~$243K)
riskFlags.detail: FDD-derived yearly gross sales of $243,355 with estimated earnings of $24,336-$29,203 - against an investment of $142,875-$276,750, the average store is a thin single-operator income, not a manage-the-manager asset.
riskFlags.source: Techy 2023 FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter review; Vetted Biz)

riskFlags.title: Dollar-minimum fees are heavy at low volumes
riskFlags.detail: Marketing/technology fund is the greater of 2% of gross revenues or $785/month, and local marketing the greater of 1.5% or $500/month, on top of a 7% weekly royalty (6% above $7,500/week) - at the ~$243K average volume, minimums push effective ongoing fees well above 10% of revenue.
riskFlags.source: Techy FDD fee disclosures (via Franchise Chatter 2023 review)

riskFlags.title: Hyper-growth young system (+173.8% units)
riskFlags.detail: Entrepreneur shows 167 units with a +173.8% trend - most locations are brand new, rebranded from Dr Phone Fix, or opened by first-time multi-unit owners, so long-run unit survivability is unproven; device-repair demand also trends down as phone prices/durability and trade-in programs improve.
riskFlags.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory (Techy)

totalInvestmentMin.note: Franchise Chatter's 2023 FDD review cited $142,875-$276,750; Entrepreneur's current range is $112,500-$399,500.

franchiseFee.source: Entrepreneur Franchise 500 directory; Franchise Chatter 2023 FDD review

royaltyPercent.source: Techy FDD (via Franchise Chatter 2023 review); Entrepreneur
royaltyPercent.note: 7% weekly; drops to 6% in weeks where gross revenues reach $7,500.

adFundPercent.note: Greater of 2% or $785/month marketing+technology fund, plus greater of 1.5% or $500/month local marketing.

avgUnitRevenue.source: Techy FDD Item 19 (via Franchise Chatter 2023 review; Vetted Biz)

yearFounded.note: Originally operated as Dr Phone Fix before the Techy rebrand.

thirdPartyFinancing.value: Third-party financing available per Entrepreneur directory

businessModelType.source: Techy franchisor site (consumer repair plus business IT/device services)

fddAvailable.source: Franchise Chatter 2023 FDD review; Vetted Biz

item19Available.source: Franchise Chatter 2023 FDD review (average sales disclosed)

brands.name: Fix Auto USA
brands.notes: Collision-repair (body shop) franchise founded by Erick and Shelly Bickett in 1997, West Coast-concentrated, acquired by Driven Brands in April 2020 and folded into its Paint, Collision & Glass segment alongside CARSTAR, Maaco, and ABRA. Mostly a conversion play: existing body-shop owners rebrand to access insurance direct-repair (DRP) volume - which is why Item 7 spans $170K (conversion) to $3.09M (ground-up). Strong $2.39M AUV.

riskFlags.title: Parent Driven Brands runs competing collision brands in the same markets
riskFlags.detail: Since the 2020 acquisition, Fix Auto USA sits inside Driven Brands' collision segment with CARSTAR, Maaco, ABRA, and company-operated shops - the franchisor's owner allocates insurance-carrier relationships and M&A capital across competing banners, not just Fix Auto.
riskFlags.source: Repairer Driven News - Driven Brands gains 150+ body shops with purchase of Fix Auto USA

riskFlags.title: Revenue depends on insurance DRP relationships
riskFlags.detail: Collision shops live on insurer direct-repair-program referrals; carriers steer volume and dictate labor rates and parts procurement, so a franchisee's $2.39M AUV potential is mediated by DRP agreements the franchisor negotiates and can lose.
riskFlags.source: SHARPSHEETS Fix Auto USA FDD analysis (business model); collision industry coverage

riskFlags.title: Extreme investment range: $170K to $3.09M
riskFlags.detail: Item 7 spans conversions of existing body shops (~$170K) to full ground-up builds (~$3.09M) - new entrants without an existing shop face capital requirements at the top of the range plus OEM-certification equipment costs.
riskFlags.source: Fix Auto USA 2025 FDD Item 7 (via SHARPSHEETS)

riskFlags.title: West Coast concentration
riskFlags.detail: The franchised network (~150-178 locations) remains concentrated in California and the West, where the brand and its insurer relationships were built; East Coast buyers get thinner brand recognition and DRP coverage.
riskFlags.source: Repairer Driven News acquisition coverage; SHARPSHEETS

liquidCapitalMin.source: SHARPSHEETS Fix Auto USA FDD analysis

brands.name: TourScale
brands.notes: Platform franchisor in 'experiential tourism' - pedal-powered party bikes and boats - rolling up five brands: Pedal Pub (60+), Cruisin' Tikis (40+, acquired early 2025), Trolley Pub (15+), Paddle Pub (8+), and Tiki Pub (6+), totaling 85+ franchise owners across 25 states. Vertically integrated (manufactures its own vehicles/vessels). Inherently seasonal and weather-dependent in most markets, with alcohol-adjacent regulatory exposure that varies city by city.

riskFlags.title: Seasonal, weather-dependent revenue
riskFlags.detail: The franchisor's own ownership models include 'Full-Time Seasonal (30-60 hrs/week in season)' - party-bike and tiki-boat tours are concentrated in warm months and good weather in most of the 25 states, while royalties and fixed costs run year-round.
riskFlags.source: TourScale franchise page (franchisor ownership models)

riskFlags.title: No Item 19 revenue claims published; young roll-up structure
riskFlags.detail: TourScale publishes no financial performance representation on its franchise site, and the platform itself is newly assembled (Cruisin' Tikis acquired in early 2025; Pedal Pub added to the platform) - brand-level FDDs, support teams, and fee structures are still being integrated.
riskFlags.source: TourScale franchise page; Franzy/FDD Exchange TourScale records

riskFlags.title: Local alcohol, waterway, and street-use regulation gates each market
riskFlags.detail: Party bikes and booze-cruise-style tiki boats depend on municipal street-use ordinances, Coast Guard/waterway rules, and local alcohol policies; a city council or harbor authority decision can curtail operations in a territory regardless of franchisee performance.
riskFlags.source: TourScale brand model (Trolley Pub/Paddle Pub/Cruisin' Tikis operations); 2020 TourScale FDD scope

riskFlags.title: Capital-equipment-heavy investment with franchisor as vendor
riskFlags.detail: The franchisor describes the investment as ~60% capital equipment and is vertically integrated in manufacturing the bikes and vessels - franchisees buy their core assets from the franchisor, an Item 8-style supply relationship that embeds franchisor margin in equipment pricing.
riskFlags.source: TourScale franchise page (investment breakdown; vertical integration)

totalInvestmentMin.source: TourScale FDD-derived range (via The Franchise Mall and FDD Exchange)
totalInvestmentMin.note: Range spans single-vessel water concepts to multi-vehicle trolley operations.

totalInvestmentMax.source: TourScale FDD-derived range (via The Franchise Mall and FDD Exchange)

totalUnits.source: TourScale franchise page brand counts (40+ Cruisin' Tikis, 60+ Pedal Pub, 15+ Trolley Pub, 8+ Paddle Pub, 6+ Tiki Pub)
totalUnits.note: Franchisor markets '85+ franchise owners across 25 states'; brand-level location counts sum to ~129+.

yearFounded.source: TourScale site (15+ years in business; Trolley Pub launched Raleigh, NC circa 2011)
yearFounded.note: Platform entity TourScale Franchising LLC is newer; 2020 FDD covered Trolley Pub, Trolley Party, and Paddle Pub.

businessModelType.source: TourScale franchise page (consumer tour experiences; group/corporate events)

mobileBased.source: TourScale model (vehicle/vessel-based tours, no retail storefront required)

seasonalityNotes.value: Peak warm-season operations in most markets; franchisor offers a 'Full-Time Seasonal' ownership model (30-60 hrs/week in season)

internationalAvailability.source: TourScale franchise page (markets 'across the United States and beyond'; Pedal Pub has international roots)

fddAvailable.source: The FDD Exchange (TourScale/Trolley Pub/Paddle Pub FDD)

item19Available.source: TourScale franchise page (no revenue claims published); no Item 19 found in third-party FDD summaries
